topic.what: Training neural networks on traces from multiple EM probe positions to enable position-resilient profiled side-channel attacks
topic.how: Multi-place training approach where the model learns from diverse probe locations rather than single hot-spot, enabling generalization across different positions and devices
topic.why_matters: Enables reproducibility and transferability of profiled EM attacks across different labs, equipment, and chip samples without precise probe repositioning

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean rank of correct key byte (lower=better, 127.5=random)
characteristics.evidence: Tables 3-6 showing mean rank across grid points
characteristics.context: Multi-place trained models achieve mean rank 1.4-22.3 vs single hot-spot 127.5 at far positions

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean rank in cross-device evaluation setting
characteristics.context: Cross-device evaluation: SW-AES-Mun trained, SW-AES-Zrh evaluated

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of traces needed for full key disclosure in hybrid attack
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs CPA only (inf for many positions)
characteristics.evidence: Table 9, Table 10 showing HW-AES hybrid attack results
characteristics.context: Hybrid approach (NN+CPA) on hardware AES outperforms pure CPA

characteristics.what_it_measures: Leakage detection at novel probe positions not seen during training
characteristics.value: Intermittent leakage detected at grid edges
characteristics.vs_baseline: vs hot-spot model with no edge leakage
characteristics.evidence: Section 6.3 interpolation experiments with 101×101 fine grid
characteristics.context: Trained on 11×11 grid, evaluated on 101×101 fine grid showing interpolation capability

characteristics.what_it_measures: Attack success when profiling data comes from completely different lab
characteristics.evidence: Table 4 showing profiling on Munich lab data attacking Zurich desk data
characteristics.context: First profiled attack with completely independent lab datasets

novelty: First profiled attack demonstrating cross-institution transfer using completely independent labs
Multi-place neural network training for EM probe position resilience
Hybrid approach using NN as signal amplifier for classical CPA on hardware AES
Large-scale multi-position EM datasets with grid captures from 5×5 to 101×101 positions
open_problems: Hand-positioned probe experiments without precise positioning equipment
More elaborate data selection schemas combining high and low signal positions
Extension to cryptographic primitives beyond AES
Optimal number of training positions and grid density
